justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus an additional BR-5 for manager authorization). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), advance notice (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities surfaced formally in escalations (partial month accrual, retroactive window anchor, balance re-validation at approval). 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, and more. Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated given the identified gaps.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classifications — no misclassifications
Excellent invariant justifications explaining why each rule is or is not a structural constraint vs. configurable policy
12 acceptance criteria far exceeding the minimum 4, with thorough coverage of accrual, negative balance, advance notice, medical leave, rejection, contract boundary, and carryover
Strong ambiguity surfacing: partial month accrual, retroactive window anchor date, and balance re-validation at approval time — all with concrete options and recommendations
Additional BR-5 (manager authorization) shows domain thinking beyond the minimum expected rules
Edge cases are exceptionally thorough — cross-year leave, pending requests during forfeiture, balance re-validation race condition, and 0-day request validation
Correctly identifies the 5-day retroactive window as calendar days in the ubiquitous language and acceptance criteria
Bounded context identified as single 'Leave Management' rather than the expected two contexts: 'leave lifecycle' and 'leave balance management'
Missing the expected ambiguity about in-flight leave requests when a contract ends mid-period (though contract boundary edge case partially addresses this)

trials.justification: All 7 expected rules identified (plus an extra BR-5 for manager authorization). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), negative balance (true), 3-day advance (false), medical retroactive (false), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), carryover cap (false). Multiple ambiguities surfaced including pending leave at year-end, partial month accrual, leave cancellation, and jurisdiction-specific forfeiture legality. 12 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum. Confidence level of 55 is well-calibrated given the ambiguities identified.

observations.text: All 7 required rules present with correct invariant classification — no misclassifications
Excellent invariant justifications that explain *why* each rule is or isn't an invariant
12 acceptance criteria covering accrual, negative balance, advance notice, medical leave exception, rejection, contract boundary, and carryover — far exceeding the 4 minimum
Rich ambiguity surfacing with structured options and recommendations for each
Added BR-5 (manager authorization) as a bonus rule beyond the 7 expected, correctly classified as invariant
Strong distinction between labor-law-adjacent rules and HR policy rules throughout invariant justifications
Surfaced the 'calendar days vs working days' ambiguity for the 5-day retroactive window as requested
Edge cases section covers contract-end mid-period scenario explicitly
Confidence level of 55 is appropriately calibrated — not overconfident given the genuine ambiguities

trials.justification: All 7 required rules identified (accrual rate, negative balance, 3-day advance notice, medical leave retroactive exception, rejected leave balance integrity, contract end date boundary, carryover cap) plus an additional rule (BR-5: manager-only approval). Invariant classifications are correct: accrual rate (false), carryover cap (false), negative balance (true), rejected leave (true), contract boundary (true), advance notice (false), medical leave (false). BR-5 marked invariant:true is reasonable as an access-control invariant. Multiple ambiguities surfaced (partial month accrual, 5-day window calendar vs working days, working days definition). 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria far exceeding the minimum 4, covering accrual, negative balance, medical leave exception, carryover, and more. Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated given the unspecified details noted.

observations.text: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classifications — no misclassifications
Correctly distinguished company policy (accrual rate, carryover cap, advance notice) from structural domain invariants (negative balance, balance integrity, contract boundary)
Surfaced 3 formal ambiguities as escalations with options and recommendations — exceeds the minimum of 1
13 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, covering all required scenarios plus edge cases
9 well-considered edge cases including contract end date boundary, approval-time re-validation, and pending requests at year-end
Identified an additional rule (BR-5: manager-only approval) beyond the 7 specified, showing thorough domain analysis
Business impact analysis is thorough with specific stakeholder identification and risk assessment
Confidence level of 72 is appropriate — not overconfident, with clear articulation of high/low confidence areas and explicit assumptions

trials.agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs the full lifecycle of employee leave: accrual, requesting, approval, consumption, carryover, and forfeiture. This is a payroll-adjacent domain where correctness directly affects employee compensation and labor law compliance.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The number of accrued, unconsumed leave days an employee currently holds.
